name: Votic
notes: Votic (vaďďa tšeeli), a Finnic (Uralic) language of Ingria, closely related to Estonian and Ingrian. The Latin orthography used for it follows the Finnic pattern: vowel letters a e i o u y ä ö plus ⟨õ⟩ /ɤ/ (an unrounded back/central vowel shared with Estonian) and ⟨ü⟩ /y/, doubled for phonemic length; consonants as in Finnish plus ⟨š⟩ /ʃ/, ⟨ž⟩ /ʒ/, ⟨č⟩ /t͡ʃ/ and ⟨z⟩ /z/, with ⟨v⟩ realised as the labiodental /v/ (unlike the Finnish approximant /ʋ/). Consonant length is written by doubling. Like the other Finnic languages of the area, Votic has a phonemic palatalised consonant series and palatal /ʎ ɲ ʑ/ that surface as narrow diacritics and are not resolved from the plain orthography here, so palatalisation is under-generated. Diphthong offglides are written plain. Vowel/consonant inventory and the doubled-length convention follow the Finnish base.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Votic language — Phonology and Orthography
sources.notes: Vowel inventory including /ɤ/ (õ) and /y/ (ü), the sibilant/affricate series /ʃ ʒ tʃ/, phonemic quantity and palatalisation.

ancestors.notes: Finnic — the classification node this language sits under.
